title: Thelypteris calva
taxonomy: Thelypteris calva is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern species belongs to the genus Thelypteris. Thelypteris calva is a vascular plant, specifically a type of pteridophyte. It is native to Mexico and Central America. The classification of Thelypteris calva is further divided into the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Thelypteris calva is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris calva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Thelypteris calva is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
